entry.title: Getting 401 unauthorized error while trying to post using HTTP and AS2 connection from partner end
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have been encountering 401 unauthorized error while testing from partner end for HTTP and AS2 connection.

I have used the basic authentication for sender AS2 and HTTP with necessary roles assigned. Valid Certificates were updated at both ends as well.

Can anyone provide me the possible reasons for 401 error and solutions for the same?

Note - The testing when performed with same credentials using postman was successful.

 

Thank you,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
Hi ,

Yes you can do write up to correct the asset values but as I mentioned in earlier comment. Are you doing this in Dev or PRD system ? If Dev, you can play around and If its a PRD system, check with business what is their expectation and what correction they want to see in the book values.

Also regarding your  comment on Retirement without revenue - Ideally asset retirement should make asset value as zero but in your case its negative, so it could be due to incorrect asset retirement / wrong transaction type / over depreciation or manual postings. etc. 

Please check the do the asset value correction but if in PRD system, check with business before posting any correction entries

Please check and close the question if no longer required.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: how to add and populate two custom fields in addition...
entry.content: @MuditJain That's exactly what the BAdI does - it adds an additional tab on header and/or item level; custom fields can be implemented on the tabs. There is no way to add custom fields in SAP standard tabs without modifying SAP standard screens and code. My advise - use the BAdI and put the fields on the additional tabs.

entry.title: Re: Profit Center Determination Error on FOC Sale Invoice...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

If you untick the mandatory option for profit center field, it will allow you to get through the message but your profit center value will still stand with the one configured in your material master data.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Processes can now be scheduled 📅!
entry.content:
hi @Dan_Wroblewski 

Thanks for your quick response.

I was talking about process i.e 2 different process build in a same project that is released and deployed.

Now both of the process have different trigger(API and Schedule) and those 2 process were configured in single desktop agent and both process contains automations too.

You already answered my question as we can include both the process into single process with schedule trigger and also keep API trigger in parallel that will be on demand. 

I didn’t knew that process with API trigger would work if we include it in schedule trigger process like both process in one. Thanks for answering it.

entry.title: Re: Is there practice system available for course "Explor...
entry.content: Thank you for the information Margit. Hoping it will be available soon.

entry.title: Re: Getting 401 unauthorized error while trying to post u...

Hello Aditya,

Can you please share the AS2 as well as HTTP sender adapter configurations that you have ? I will try an attempt to help.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required – Agent-on-Fly Configuration for...
entry.content: Hi Srinivas, I don't see neither inceccdbgrp1 nor ineccdbgrp1 listed in the command result. According to the screenshots, the OD SHA has been enabled, please try steps in Note 2724592 - SAP Host Agent Outside Discovery ComputerSystem configuration. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Delivery block is getting removed automatically when Down Payment request is cleared
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I'm Facing one issue with regards to Down Payment Processing through Milestone billing.

I have created one Sales Order with item category CBAO, entered Billing Plan date and value at Item level and save the order. Then I created a down Payment request through VF01 and then cleared it. But I can't find any Down Payment status either at header or at item level. And the Delivery block is still there, It's not getting removed automatically upon Down Payment clearance.

Could anyone please tell me what could be the possible reasons?

Regards,

Seemita.

Can you please share a screenshot of the SOAP sender adapter please ? Let me try to help.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Sales BOM explosion with component of item category SERV
entry.content:
Dear community,

in our Public cloud system we are trying to include non-tangible subcomponent into Sales BOM (firmware product). In master data Item category group is set to SERV. Adding the product as subcomponent to main Sales BOM item works perfectly.

Problem is that this item is skipped during BOM explosion in Sales order document.

I tried to assign different Item type when setting up Sales BOM, but result is same. In blogs I read that there are limitations for CPFH scenario and DISH scenario but in our case we would like to use either ERLA or LUMF cases.

"Limitations for variant CPFH:

  • Scenarios with 3rd party items, bought in items and service materials are not supported."

    Do you have any suggestion how to resolve this issue?

    Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Getting 401 unauthorized error while trying to post u...
entry.content: Hi Punith, Updated the adapter configurations in the post (Used the TPM AS2 sender channel)

entry.title: FS Item with Formula - Group Reporting S/4HANA cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are implementing S/4HANA cloud 202502, No GRDC 

Wanted some suggestion from the experts on some reporting requirement in group reporting for our client. 

We basically need to show some line items in the P&L statement which would be derived based on formulas, for eg: Contribution Expense % to Revenue, Operating cost % to Revenue, EBITDA which is based on other FS items. 

How do we achieve this in group reporting? 

Hope to get some advice.

 

Thank you

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

entry.title: CALL TRANSFORMATION - Existence check condition on XML elements
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a situation where I want to parse an XML file using a simple XSLT. I use conditions on tags in case an element is not present in the source XML.

The problem is that it seems in some elements the conditional check works and in other it fails. 

If I omit the conditional check and just read the value of the element - if present - it reads it ok.

I attach an extract of the XSLT source code to let you understand and, possibly, help me overcome this issue. Also, I attach part of the XML under deserialization.

Thanks a lot,

Konstantinos

 

NB I found the solution, by mistake I was using the wrong ST command. Since I am doing de-serialization, I had to use the command shown in the last attachment.

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 基準線の作成
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能をつかってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、データが平均や目標などの基準を上回っているのか、あるいは下回っているのかを一目でわかるようにしたい際にご使用いただける基準線の挿入についてご紹介いたします。

 

 

目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudにおける基準線の概要
  • 基準線の作成方法 [ハンズオン]
    • 固定基準線の作成
    • 動的基準線の作成
    • 背景色の作成

※ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成⼿順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。
※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画⾯キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2025年5⽉に作成しています。）

 

SAP Analytics Cloudにおける基準線の概要

SAP Analytics Cloudでは、製品の平均価格や最高価格などの重要な値をチャートに表示するために、基準線を定義することができます。基準線には固定と動的の2種類があります。

固定基準線は特定の参照値を使用して作成され、チャートのデータを変更しても変化しないため、特定の値や目標、閾値を示す際に便利です。例えば、売上チャートに予算目標を示すことで、現在の売上が目標に対してどの位置にあるかを視覚的に確認できます。

動的基準線は、データの傾向を把握したり、リアルタイムでの比較分析を行う際に便利です。動的基準線は、データセットに基づいてリアルタイムで更新されるため、平均値や中央値など、変化のある値を参照したい際に使用できます。市場や環境の変化に対応する必要がある場合も、動的基準線を使用いただくことで、リアルタイムなデータの把握が可能となります。

基準線を作成したら、基準線を超えているか否かで背景色を作成することができます。背景色を作成することで、より基準線を越えているかが分かりやすいチャートを作成することができます。

詳しくはHelp Portalもご参照ください：リファレンスライン | SAP Help Portal

 

基準線の作成方法

0. （リンク）で作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1 ナビゲーションバーのファイルより作成したストーリーを開き、「編集」をクリックします。

 

1. 固定基準線を作成しよう！

1.1. 基準線を追加したいチャートを選択し、ビルダパネルを開きます。
今回は事業セグメントごとの売上高を選択してください。

 

1.2. メニュー内、チャートアドオンから「基準線」を選択します。
※チャートアドオンはメニュー内の下の方にあるので画面に表示されていない場合はスクロールダウンして探してください。

 

 

1.3. 参照したい値と参照ラベルを入力します。
タイプは「固定」を選択、値に「60,000,000」を入力します。

 

 

1.4. 固定基準線を作成することができました。

 

2. 動的基準線を作成しよう！

2.1. 先ほどの基準線のメニューに戻ります。タイプは「動的」を選択し、参照したいメジャーを選択します。売上高の平均を基準線とするため、メジャーとして「売上高」を選択します。

 

2.2. 集計方法を入力します。集計方法は「平均」を入力します。

 

2.3. 動的基準線を作成することができました。

 

 

3. 背景色を作成しよう！

 

3.1. 基準線メニュー内の「上回る：」「下回る：」から色を選択します。平均を超える領域を緑色・超えない領域を灰色に設定します。

 

3.2. 基準線より高い領域に色を付けることができました。

 

今回は基準線のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: BOM recursive (message no. 29110)
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: Fiori Reuse Library
entry.content:
Hello @Hapu 

Thank you for sharing this valuable use case.

Since the app runs in BAS but fails after deployment to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the issue likely arises from missing authorisations or incorrect scope assignments for the Reuse Library.
Please check:

• Business roles/catalogues for access to Reuse Library components (e.g., Change Documents).
• manifest.json to ensure correct componentUsages and crossNavigation settings.
• Deployment via Maintain Extensions and Custom Catalogue Extensions apps.

If the issue persists, consider logging a ticket under component CA-FLP-FE-EXT.
Looking forward to your update; your findings may help others too.
Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Delivery block is getting removed automatically when...
entry.content: It is not sure whether the full amount is cleared and updated in FI table.  Have you checked in FBL5N with the selection option "Cleared items" ?  Check this help documentation

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content:
SAP Inventory Valuation in Public Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support using the same moving average price for sales order stock and normal inventory stock simultaneously. The system handles these two stock types differently:

  1. Normal inventory stock uses the moving average price method, which calculates a new material price after each receipt by dividing the total stock value by the total quantity in stock .
  2. For sales order stock, SAP recommends not using the moving average price. Instead, the standard price method is preferred for internally manufactured materials [1].
  3. When using valuated sales order stock, the inventory values are updated when goods receipts are entered for internally manufactured or externally procured materials [1].
  4. For sales order stock managed on a quantity and value basis:
    • The standard price is updated by the first goods receipt posting
    • The moving price may change as a result of goods movements and invoices, and can be updated statistically [1]
    • The system calculates the moving average price for sales order stock automatically by dividing the material value in the material stock account by the total of all warehouse inventories in a plant [1].

      It's important to note that while both stock types can use moving average prices, they are calculated and managed separately. The public cloud version does not provide a built-in feature to use the same moving average price across both stock types simultaneously.

      For specific requirements or customizations, it's advisable to consult with SAP support or an SAP implementation partner to explore potential solutions within the constraints of the public cloud environment

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content: SAP Inventory Valuation in Public Cloud SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support using the same moving average price for sales order stock and normal inventory stock simultaneously. The system handles these two stock types differently: Normal inventory stock uses the moving average price method, which calculates a new material price after each receipt by dividing the total stock value by the total quantity in stock . For sales order stock, SAP recommends not using the moving average price. Instead, the standard price method is preferred for internally manufactured materials [1]. When using valuated sales order stock, the inventory values are updated when goods receipts are entered for internally manufactured or externally procured materials [1]. For sales order stock managed on a quantity and value basis: The standard price is updated by the first goods receipt posting The moving price may change as a result of goods movements and invoices, and can be updated statistically [1] The system calculates the moving average price for sales order stock automatically by dividing the material value in the material stock account by the total of all warehouse inventories in a plant [1]. It's important to note that while both stock types can use moving average prices, they are calculated and managed separately. The public cloud version does not provide a built-in feature to use the same moving average price across both stock types simultaneously. For specific requirements or customizations, it's advisable to consult with SAP support or an SAP implementation partner to explore potential solutions within the constraints of the public cloud environment

entry.title: Re: Profit Center Determination Error on FOC Sale Invoice...
entry.content:
Hi @VPS80 

This error comes when Billing document don't get profit center as profit center is mandatory at billing document and it should be mandatory. What you need to check is below -

1) Check if profit center is assigned in material master. Profit center in Sales order flows from Material Master so its important to maintain profit center in Material master.

2) Check if Sales Order has Profit center, if not, assign and release again sales billing document through VF02. 

3) If above 2 don't work, you need to check your document splitting configuration. Also check extended document splitting settings if everything maintained properly or not.

Do as mentioned above and confirm. This should work as its a very common error faced by many users.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content: SAP Inventory Valuation in Public Cloud SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support using the same moving average price for sales order stock and normal inventory stock simultaneously. The system handles these two stock types differently: Normal inventory stock uses the moving average price method, which calculates a new material price after each receipt by dividing the total stock value by the total quantity in stock . For sales order stock, SAP recommends not using the moving average price. Instead, the standard price method is preferred for internally manufactured materials [1]. When using valuated sales order stock, the inventory values are updated when goods receipts are entered for internally manufactured or externally procured materials [1]. For sales order stock managed on a quantity and value basis: The standard price is updated by the first goods receipt posting The moving price may change as a result of goods movements and invoices, and can be updated statistically [1] The system calculates the moving average price for sales order stock automatically by dividing the material value in the material stock account by the total of all warehouse inventories in a plant [1]. It's important to note that while both stock types can use moving average prices, they are calculated and managed separately. The public cloud version does not provide a built-in feature to use the same moving average price across both stock types simultaneously. For specific requirements or customizations, it's advisable to consult with SAP support or an SAP implementation partner to explore potential solutions within the constraints of the public cloud environment

entry.title: Re: Migration of "Routing" Master data(with Inspection Characteristics) in Rise with SAP

"The component to be assigned has not been completely specified."

entry.title: Re: Down Payment Settlement Line Items Repeating in Final...
entry.content:
Dear SAP

Actually, the question is the Downpayment settlement item showing again in Manage customer line item app. 

1. once generate the downpayment request from SD Billing, FI will clear the request thru post outgoing payment APP,  Then this payment reflected in customer ledger open items (Manage Customer line item app) KZ document. 

2. After that that generating the invoice from Billing screen, that time again the Downpayment settlement was run and reflected to customer ledger open items (Customer line item app).

How to clear these items automatically ? or how to stop the downpayment settlement at the time of invoice ?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cold Data Management - BW on HANA and ADLS Integr...
entry.content: Not sure about Azure but I know Datasphere has more cost effective storage in the object store.

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content:
Hi Lina,

I do have some quest regarding the structure above. 

"Association to different dimension/text views – It is a relation from the nodes and leaves to dimension and text views. Via this association the text of a node/leaf is derived. In the following example '_node' is first level node, '_airline' acts as second level node and '_connection' is leaf and pointing to dimension"

do I need to associated each level of node?

If I say _airline is multilevel. How should I do it?

would greatly appriciate your help!

Best Regards

Tao

entry.title: Re: How to run ATC checks only on code delta of a changed...
entry.content:
Hi, 

as far as I know this is not possible. ATC always checks the whole object. 

It is possible in certain cases to display only parts of the result list. One of these cases is when checking a transport. But the list is filtered on subobject level. Subobject means all LIMU objects in a transport. So you can show only one method of a class but not only one Form of an include.

This setting requires some flags in ATC and ideally a System with a SAP_Basis Level as high as possible (I don't remember when these changes where shipped but it is not very long ago).

On the other hand: Why don't you want these results? In german there is something translated word by word as "boy scout rule": always leave the place you were camping a tiny bit better than it was when you arrived. And ATC always showing all result helps fulfilling that rule.

Hope that helps. 

Michael

entry.title: Re: Recycle Bin Does Not Show All Records, Shows Blank Pages
entry.content:
Hi,

You should try the SAP notes :

1267338 - How to use Repository Diagnostic Tool (reposcan)
1689163 - Using the Repository Diagnostic Tool (aka reposcan) in BI 4.x - for WINDOWS [How-To]
2521284 - Repository Diagnostic Tool (RepoScan) ****LEAD KBA****
2641395 - How to trace reposcan
 
Regards

entry.title: Re: Error in Customer Master Migration in Migrate your Data
entry.content:
Hi @danny_loo 

You are getting error because you are maintain same external number CCPP01KEY in both the tabs " Main Address and Additional Address". External number should be different in Main and Additional address tab.

Maintain different external number in Additional Address tab and reupload. It will work. 

Please check and confirm

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Monitoring the resource consumption of seamless planning in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:

Clear, Crisp, Simple and Effective.

With your blog, I may have found the "missing piece of a puzzle" 😀. Reference to the question I posted in this blog

Regards, Sai Kiran Reddy Arva

entry.title: Re: Getting 401 unauthorized error while trying to post u...
entry.content: Hello Aditya, One solution could be to create a service key using the sender system's SSL certificate. You can contact your BTP administrator to help you create the same. 1. Log in to the SAP BTP cockpit. Navigate to "Services Instances and Subscriptions". Select the relevant service instance. In the "Service Keys" section, click "Create". 2. Creating the Service Key: In the "New Service Key" dialog: Enter a name for the service key. Choose "Certificate" as the "Key Type". Paste the sender's certificate (External Certificate) into the designated field.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Banking Security with SAP Integration Suite: JWS, JWE, and mTLS
entry.content: Hi @felipe_correa , Thank you for this blog. I am trying to upload the nimbus jar file to the script collection and added to the iflow reference also, but still getting error while using the classes in the groovy script . could you please help ?

entry.title: Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：业务伙伴Business Partner主数据
entry.content:
这篇文章是作为Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：产品主数据 的后续，介绍第二个集成场景：业务伙伴Business Partner主数据。

 

1. 业务伙伴Business Partner的集成：

BP主数据的集成，我们参考的是同一个scope item 1RO（Master Data Integration）。

1.1 发送系统的设置

1.1.1 创建通信系统

与前一个场景一样，我们同样需要首先设置通信系统。该通信系统是需要集成的目标系统。

输入自定义的System ID、System Name，Host Name为接收系统（receiver system）的地址，维护字段Logical System和Business System。

另外，需要在通信系统中维护通信的inbound user和outbound user。

这里需要特别注意的是，发送系统的outbound user与之后维护的接收系统的inbound user需要一致。

1.1.2 创建通信安排

BP主数据的集成借助了通信场景SAP_COM_0008，创建基于该通信场景的通信安排，选中之前创建的通信系统：

具体使用到的接口信息如下，选择激活该SOAP接口，并且维护Replication Model， Replication Mode， Output Mode。

 

1.2 接收系统的设置

1.2.1 通信系统的设置

不同于发送系统中创建的通信系统需要选择接收系统，在接收系统中创建的通信系统是选择其本身，因为在这个通信场景里，它是一个被动接收的角色。

同样，在该通信系统中，需要维护inbound user和outbound user。 inbound user需要特别注意，与之前发送系统的outbound user维护成一致。

 

1.2.2 创建通信安排

接收端的通信安排不需要激活outbound services，使用的是下面的这个inbound service。因此，不需要特别的设置。

 

1.3 设置消息的监控

为了测试中能够看到SOAP接口的消息，打开应用Assign Recipients to Users，给测试的用户分配以下条目：

 

1.4 测试

与前一篇分享的类似，BP主数据的集成可以单个集成同步（Replicate by Object Selection）或者批量同步完成（Replicate by Replication Model）。具体可参考 Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：产品主数据

 

1.5 查看消息日志

消息的查看同样是在Message Dashboard里可以看到。

 

2. 参考文档

如文章开头所说，实现Cloud to cloud的业务伙伴Business Partner主数据的集成，参考的是标准scope item 1RO，如想了解详细内容，可参考以下链接：

1RO（Master Data Integration）的set-up Guide

通过该标准流程，亦可实现BP主数据在cloud系统之间通过后台作业的方式实现数据的同步。

感谢阅读！

entry.title: Understanding Subsidies for Workers Over 62 with Training Contracts in Spain
entry.content:
We have  had some issues with the way to calculate subsidies/reductions in the case of employees over 62 and sickness when they have alternating training contracts as we have seen different ways of doing by Social Security and would like to learn how it should be. Here you have the question we did and answer provided by authorities.
 
Question
We would like to confirm with you what needs to be subsidised when there is a 75% subsidy applicable to workers over 62 years old in a situation of temporary disability and who also have a training contract in alternation 421 and TRLL 087.
We have two scenarios with employees aged 62 or older with the training contract in alternation (421 and TRL 087).
When these employees are sick, the 75% subsidy should be calculated on the common contingency contribution quotas of workers over 62 years old in a situation of temporary disability (1062).
 
1.- Employee has 18 days of absenteeism, and it is expected that the 1062 subsidy will be calculated from the excess base of absenteeism:
Absenteeism base: 48.65 18 = 875.70
Minimum base: 44.1 18 = 793.8
Excess base: 875.70 - 793.8 = 81.9
Excess quota: 81.9 * 23.6% = 19.3284
Subsidy 1062 = 19.3284 * 75% = 14.50
 
2.- Employee has absenteeism throughout the month, the calculation file expects the subsidy to be calculated from the entire employee's quota, which includes the fixed quota of the training contract in alternation and the quota of the excess base:
Absenteeism base: 1459.50.
Minimum base of the month: 1381.20
Excess base: 1459.50 - 1381.20 = 78.3
Excess quota: 78.3 * 23.6% = 18.4788
Fixed quota = 55.97
Total quota = 55.97 + 18.48 = 74.45
Subsidy 1062 = 74.45 * 75% = 55.84
 
These scenarios seem contradictory because the fixed quota is only used when absenteeism occurs throughout the month.
Could you help us determine how we should perform the calculation? Why are we receiving calculations subsidising the fixed quota in some cases and not in others?
 
Answer
In the second option, we understand that it refers to the liquidation period 03/2025, given that the L00 liquidation for the period 04/2025 has not been presented.
In the 2025 Contribution Order in article 46, it indicates regarding the training contract in alternation:
 
"From January 1, 2025, the contribution to Social Security and for other protected contingencies regarding workers who have entered into a training and learning contract or a training contract in alternation will be carried out according to the following rules: First. When the monthly contribution base for common contingencies, determined according to the rules established in the corresponding Social Security regime, does not exceed the minimum monthly contribution base of said regime:
  1. The contribution to Social Security will consist of a single monthly quota of 67.13 euros for common contingencies, of which 55.97 euros will be borne by the employer and 11.16 euros by the worker, and 7.71 euros for professional contingencies, borne by the employer, of which 3.99 euros correspond to temporary disability and 3.72 euros to permanent disability and death and survival.
  2. When the monthly contribution base for common contingencies, determined according to the rules established in the corresponding Social Security regime, exceeds the minimum monthly contribution base of said regime, the single quotas referred to in the first rule will be added to the quotas resulting from applying, to the amount by which the contribution base exceeds the minimum base, the following contribution rates"
The fixed quota, being unique, is calculated in the first segment of the worker.
Therefore, in the 2nd case, liquidation period 03/2025, the worker has a single segment for the entire month with absenteeism. Therefore, it is calculated with the fixed quota plus the quota of the excess base, and the 75% reduction of the employer's quota for common contingencies is applied to the total.
 
In the 1st case, liquidation period 09/2024, the worker has 3 segments with the following situations:
Segment 1: 1-12 Active
Segment 2: 13-27 IT Absenteeism
Segment 3: 13-27 IT Absenteeism
In the first segment, in the worker's active situation, the fixed quota is calculated. In the 2nd and 3rd segments, there is no fixed quota calculation, only the calculation of the excess base quota. The 75% reduction of the employer's quota for common contingencies is applied to the excess base.
 
Previous answer continue with an additional comment as reductions will be different if the absenteeism is at the begining, at the end or in the middle.
Additional Question
An additional comment, then if an employee with this type of contract (intern) initially has a temporary disability and then is active. If the fixed quota is applied in the first segment, would it be subsidized for the entire quota, is that correct? But if it is the last one, it doesn't. Are we right?
 
Answer
If the first segment has a temporary disability, in this segment the 75% reduction would be applied to the fixed quota plus the excess base quota. If there is a temporary disability in segments other than the first, the reduction is only applied to the excess base quota.
 
That confirms that the reduction is only applied in segment with sickness and fixed quota is only benefit when it happens at the beginning of the period when it is calculated.

entry.title: SBM closing bill process is taking more than expected
entry.content:
Hi experts!

From time to time, we encounter a problem with the process of manually closing a bill via the API. It takes more than 10 seconds for the bill to reach the status CLOSED. Is there an expected limit time for this process? Is there anything we can do when sending the request to speed up this action?

Thanks in advance!

BR,

Alberto.

entry.title: Re: Deep Link in Story Reports for Performance Forms
entry.content: A highly informative and helpful read

entry.title: How to extract a list of the users of who have not created and maintained a substitute for approvals
entry.content:
Dear Sap Team, 

I am trying to extract a list of the users of who have created and maintained a substitute for approvals (planned/unplanned) in the S/4HANA Public Cloud as we have several times faced issues when user delegation in MyInbox is not maintained. How can I as Admin create such a list to contact people who did not add a substitute?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to extract a list of the users of who have not cr...
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

Currently there is no app specific to this to view the list of users who have not maintained a substitute. 

You can however make use of the Released CDS views like  I_WorkflowStatusOverview, I_WorkflowStatusDetails, I_UserSubstitute and I_WorkflowRecipients. You can join these using custom analytical query which can be used to build your custom report. 

Best Regards.

entry.title: Depreciations of assets in foreign currency
entry.content:
Dear colleagues, 

I would like to ask whether there is a solution to maintaining depreciation areas in foreign currency that would post the depreciation in other currency other than the one set up for Company Code. 

Current setup - Chart of depreciation has one 01 leading Depreciation area in IFRS and one 10 in GAAP.  Company code currency is PHP, and client needs to do the depreciation in EUR and USD. we tried setting up the parallel currency based on the knowledge article Parallel Currencies in depreciation areas , where based on this article the postings are translated to Free Defined Currency 1 and Free Defined Currency 2, and depreciation areas in EUR and USD are set up as Areas Do not Post,   but this solution is not feasible for the client as they want to lower the acquisition value if in EUR by only in EUR, and this solution gives only overview of the depreciation translated to EUR and the depreciation is done only in PHP.  

 

if acqusition is in EUR document currency and PHP company code currency the provided solution with new depreciation area for EUR give us right amounts for depreciation calculation but without actual posted amounts. The posting of depreciation is still only in leading depreciation area 01 with translation to free defined currencies EUR and USD which is not accepted by customer.

As per my understanding, under one accounting principle there can be only 1 leading depreciation are which posts in real time therefore EUR and USD areas would need to be set up as post depreciation only, here is a catch, the client wants to post the depreciation to the same GL accounts in 01 IFRS PHP and 20 IFRS EUR and 30 IFRS  USD. Ideally client want to choose that if Acquired Assets' document currency is in EUR, and company code currency is PHP, then the posting should be done only  in depreciation area for EUR, where posting of depreciation document currency is EUR and company code currency is PHP. 

Is there any reasonable solution for this? 

Thank you in advance. 

Best regards,

Lenka

entry.title: How to Manage Third-Party and Individual Purchase Order Procurement Processes in SAP SD-MM
entry.content:
In today’s customer-centric business environment, organisations must ensure the efficient fulfilment of customer demands, even when products or services are not available in-house. This is where Third-Party Procurement and Individual Purchase Order processes in SAP play a vital role. These functionalities, part of the seamless integration between SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) and Materials Management (MM) modules, enable companies to procure goods directly from external vendors to meet customer requirements without holding inventory.

This blog aims to provide a clear understanding of how SAP handles third-party order processing and individual purchase orders, highlighting the underlying configuration, document flow, and business benefits.

Step 1: Configuration Steps

Maintain Purchase Organisation

Path: SPRO -> Enterprise Structure -> Definition -> Materials Management -> Maintain purchasing organization

Step 2: Assign Purchase Organisation

Path: SPRO -> Enterprise Structure -> Assignment -> Materials Management -> Assign purchasing organisation to company code

Path: SPRO -> Enterprise Structure -> Assignment -> Materials Management -> Assign purchasing organisation to plant

Step 3: Assign Item Categories

Path: SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales -> Sales Documents -> Sales Document Item -> Assign Item Categories

Step 4: Create a customer Record (Transaction: BP)

You can refer link below and follow the suggested steps to create a BP record.

Business Partner(BP) Complete Configuration Guide in SAP S4 HANA

Step 5: Create Material Master Record (Transaction: MM01)

Maintain the General Item Category group and Item category group data in the sales org. view 2

Step 6: Define Schedule Line Categories

Path: SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales -> Sales Documents -> Schedule Lines -> Define Schedule Line Categories

Order Type: It enables the automatic generation of a purchase requisition in the background during the creation of a sales order in a third-party process.

Item Category: In the third-party procurement process, the PR is generated automatically in the background. To facilitate this, the system derives and passes the required item category information from the schedule line category to the purchase requisition.

The item category 5 ensures that when MIGO is performed in a third-party scenario with a shipping notification, the system treats it as a dummy goods movement, meaning the stock is not updated in inventory.

Step 7: Define Shipping Points

Path: SPRO -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order - > Set up STO -> Define shipping points

Step 8: Create Sales Order (Transaction: VA01)

Check for PR Number in Display Sales Order

Step 9: Convert Purchase Request into Purchase Order (Transaction: ME21N) 

Check the Sales Document process flow

Step 10: Post Incoming Invoice (Transaction: MIRO) 

 

Individual Purchase Order Process

The IPO process is triggered during sales order creation when a specific item category (e.g., TAB) is assigned. This item category configuration signals the system to create a Purchase Requisition (PR) automatically, linking it to the sales order item. Unlike standard procurement, the goods procured are not stocked for general use - they are tied exclusively to that customer order.

Step 1: Assign Item Categories

Path: SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales -> Sales Documents -> Sales Document Item -> Assign Item Categories

Step 2: Create Material Master Record (Transaction: MM01)

Maintain the General Item Category group and Item category group data in the sales org. view 2

Step 3: Define Schedule Line Categories

Path: SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales -> Sales Documents -> Schedule Lines -> Define Schedule Line Categories

Step 4: Create Sales Order (Transaction: VA01) 

Check the PR Number in the Display Sales Order

Step 5: Convert PR into PO (Transaction: ME21N)

Refer to step 9 above for more detailed steps

Step 6: Check Stock (Transaction: MMBE)

Step 7: Post Goods Movement (Transaction: MIGO)

Check stock

Step 8: Post Incoming Invoice (Transaction: MIRO)

PO History

 

Regards,

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: SAP Sandbox
entry.content: Hi - new to this so please excuse if a naive question (!) - My company has a SaaS system that allows our users to record their carbon usage across their organisation facilitating mandatory reporting on carbon emissions. We have a requirement to automatically pull in BOM, Supplier, Supplier Email and PCF data relating to BOM items, if applicable, from SAP 4/HANA. We would need to subsequently synchronise the data - so if anything changed in the BOM etc. the SaaS system would remain in sync. We could poll the SAP system to look for changes but I believe we could also be notified of any updates using SAP Event Mesh ? Are there any SAP sandbox systems - on premise version and cloud version - where we could look to develop this functionality from our side - access the relevant API's, populate with test data etc.?

entry.title: Re: SAP SF EC WF Email Notifications
entry.content:
Hi @Xavier_Volks 

In SF, transaction workflow is a standard and it routes the transaction based on the Approver type and Approver role . The workflow email notification content can be standard or customised. 

When we define workflow and the role, there are 3 sections a. Approver b. Contributor c. CC role.

When initiator initiates the request, in the particular transaction if workflow is mapped then it triggers a communication to the approver based on the approver type and role defined. In "Workflow Email Configuration" if custom email content is defined then approver will receive the custom content else standard email content will be notified. If there is no workflow defined in the transaction then the request will get deemed approval.

As soon as the approver actions the transaction then in the CC role if we define role for initiator then initiator will be notified on the approvers action. This email content also be customised. 

Contributor can able to view the transaction and can able to only post the comment.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Deciding region/data center for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
I think we can select Cloud ALM region in multiple region now.
And I think region option is expanding from now on.

But I am not sure how to choose region of Cloud ALM.
Please tell me consideration for deciding one or differences of function between regions.
How we should decide region?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Deciding region/data center for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In SAP for Me, the optimal region is selected for you by default, based on factors such as latency and data center utilization. However, you can also make a different selection from the drop-down menu, which offers a complete list of all regions that are currently available for SAP Cloud ALM.

Supported Data Centers for SAP Cloud ALM 

In general, we recommend selecting a region that is close to your other main business applications, especially the ones you're planning to manage with SAP Cloud ALM for operations.

To find out which data center locations are planned in the future, see SAP Cloud ALM Data Centers on SAP Support Portal.

entry.title: Re: How to track the person responsible or custodian of t...
entry.content: In Sap private cloud there is an option to enable the personnel number field in the time dependent data, how can i do that in the public cloud?

entry.title: Re: How to run ATC checks only on code delta of a changed...
entry.content:
You can have a look on how to configure a baseline for your existing code base, or create exemptions to suppress some findings. However this doesn't prevent the ATC to check the whole object, it is more of a setting to hide some already known findings, and as BiberM said, you should clean up you existing code and don't use this as a way to just ignore those existing findings.

Have a look on this documentation, maybe it is helpful in your case:
https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/ba879a6e2ea04d9bb94c7ccd7cdac446/59513bfbf0084cb88386bd8f07d03efb.html?locale=en-US&version=202110.latest

entry.title: Re: SAP b1 10.0 Hana Fiori Cockpit Icon Change Issue
entry.content:
Dear @kbasher , 

Here is a screenshot from version 10 2502, as you can see issue is solved:

Regards,

Maya

entry.title: Re: Job title not appearing on external job posting on ca...
entry.content:
Hi @Nim_18 ,

1. Field Mapping Issue in Job Field Mapping (RCM to RMK)
The job title field might not be properly mapped between RCM and RMK via the Job Field Mapping Tool in Provisioning.

Even though it appears in the Command Center export, if it's not properly mapped, it may not render on the external RMK site.

Login to  Provisioning > Configure Recruiting Marketing Job Field Mapping.

Ensure that the title field from RCM is correctly mapped to RMK’s title field.

2. Career Site Builder (CSB) Template Issue
If you're using Career Site Builder, it’s possible the job listing or job detail layout does not include the title token.

What to check:

Go to CSB > Pages > Job Detail or Job Layout Template.

Make sure the [[TITLE]] token is included in the layout/template.

3. Caching or Sync Delay
Sometimes, RMK can have delayed syncs or caching issues.

The data may appear correctly in previews or CSVs but still not be fully pushed to the external site due to incomplete job sync.

Try below:

Trigger a full job sync manually from Command Center or Recruiting Posting.

Wait up to an hour and re-check.

4. Localization or Language Handling
If the job is posted in a non-default locale, and the title isn’t filled for that locale, RMK might hide the title instead of falling back.

check below option:

Ensure that the job title is populated for all required locales (especially the site's default language).

Check if any locale-specific configuration is hiding the title.

Hope this helps!! kindly let me know if you need any assistance.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: PDF Payload logging in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have got a requirement to log the PDF payload at the time of exception. I have tried the below script but the payload that got logged was not in PDF format but in .txt format.

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
 
def Message processData(Message message)
{
def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String) as String;
def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
 
if(messageLog != null)
{
messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Payload", body, "application/pdf");
}
 
return message;
}
 
Can anyone please provide a solution to this issue.
 
Thank You,
Aditya

entry.title: Re: Inter Company STO Process in S/4 HANA

Can someone explain which condition type will hold the value in MM and SD pricing Procedure, Do we need to maintain the same condition type at both pricing procedure?

entry.title: How to implement hierarchical filtering in TreeTable in SAPUI5?
entry.content:
I'm using the sap.ui.table.TreeTable in SAPUI5 to display hierarchical data (like multi-level BOMs). I want to implement filtering so that when a child node matches the filter, its parents are still visible to maintain the tree structure.

Issue:

Currently, when I use standard filtering (via filter Property on columns), it only filters visible rows. If a match is found in a nested/child node that is collapsed, it doesn’t show up, and its parent is not included either.

Expected Behavior:

If a child or deeper node matches the filter condition:

  • The entire path (i.e., parent → child) should be visible

  • Tree structure should be preserved

  • Filter should work recursively on all levels

Actual Behavior:

  • Filtering only applies to currently visible rows

  • Deep nested matches are ignored unless parent is expanded

  • Filtered result appears empty, even when matching values exist

What I Tried:

I implemented a custom recursive filter on the JSON model before binding to the TreeTable:


This works partially, but:

  • Breaks expand/collapse logic

  • Loses lazy-loading benefits

  • Not ideal for large datasets

My Questions:

  1. Is there a standard way to implement hierarchical filtering in Tree table?

  2. Can Tree table support full-depth filtering while maintaining structure?

  3. Are there any recommended workarounds or best practices?

  4. Is this feature on SAP’s roadmap?

Thanks in advance! Open to all suggestions, links, or alternative approaches.

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:m="sap.m">

    <table:TreeTable
        id="idTreeTable"
        rows="{
            path: 'myModel>/Items',
            parameters: {
                arrayNames: ['Children']
            }
        }"
        selectionMode="MultiToggle"
        enableColumnReordering="true"
        alternateRowColors="true"
        visibleRowCount="10">

        <table:columns>
            <table:Column filterProperty="Name" width="20%">
                <m:Label text="Name"/>
                <table:template>
                    <m:Text text="{myModel>Name}" />
                </table:template>
            </table:Column>

            <table:Column filterProperty="Type" width="15%">
                <m:Label text="Type"/>
                <table:template>
                    <m:Text text="{myModel>Type}" />
                </table:template>
            </table:Column>
        </table:columns>

    </table:TreeTable>

</core:FragmentDefinition>

entry.title: Re: SAP SF EC Associations
entry.content:
Hi @Xavier_Volks 

In EC there are 2 different elements, Standard and custom elements.

Standard Elements are part of Manage Business Configuration, which are SAP provided elements and Custom Elements are part of Manage Object Definitions, we shall able to create multiple custom elements.

Custom Objects are created in Manage Object Definitions as Parent and Child association. In Manage data when we create child object, we end in associating the parent object, to populate the child data items when a parent data item is selected.

In Standard Objects we are creating the couple of fields and associating towards the Generic objects (custom objects) created in Manage Object Definitions. In this only field level mapping takes place and that mapping doesn't bring the parent and child association, it will behave as a stand alone data objects.

In the Manage Business Configuration, further we have to associate the parent and child field, so it cannot be a stand alone data and it will be a dependent data on parent field selection.

Thanks & Regards

Arun

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors & Docusign Integration - Offer Letters

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Withholding taxes
entry.content: I just wanted to check if you were able to find a solution to the issue. If yes, could you kindly share the details with me? It would be really helpful.

entry.title: Re: Outdated unitNetAmount after Article sync
entry.content:
@edwin_delacruz prepaidcardstatus com wrote:

During plugin development, we've noticed that when using ReceiptPosService to obtain a ReceiptEntity for a receipt that is currently in progress, the SalesItems in the entity appear to be outdated if an Article Sync has happened recently.

We add an item that has recently changed it's price to the receipt and it continues to show the previous price. After about an hour, if we add the item again it will reflect the correct, updated price.

Has anyone come across this behavior?

 

Yes, this behavior is a known issue within SAP Customer Checkout, where ReceiptPosService provides an ReceiptEntity with outdated SalesItems prices after an Article Sync, particularly for receipts in progress. This is likely due to caching, transaction isolation, or asynchronous updates not immediately reflecting price changes in active receipts. Workarounds include re-adding items, attempting to force refresh the ReceiptEntity, or checking for "apply price changes" functionality. It's crucial to report this to SAP support and check for available patches as this is a recognized limitation.

entry.title: Do we need add-on CODERINT to be installed on SAP S4 HANA Private Cloud Edition 2023 for C4C integra

entry.title: Insert currency sign on Benefit value
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

I have currently a request from my customer to insert the € sign after Benefit value on Benefits Enrollments page.

The current request is from euro sign, but if you know how to insert the sign accordlyng employee's currency maybe is a best way.

Do you know how can we do it?

 

Best Regards,

JC

entry.title: Related For loop Iteration
entry.content:
Hello All I have table which contain suppose 10 record and in the table there is field in which i want to maintain value like  1 to 10 like counter.

        IT_FINAL_TRC = VALUE #( FOR WA_SYT0060_TRC IN IT_SYT0060_TRD_TRR_TRC (
                                    Sr_no                          = WA_SYT0060_TRC-SRNO
                                    Transport_request              = WA_SYT0060_TRC-TRPRN
                                    Transport_Request_description  = WA_SYT0060_TRC-TRPRD
                                    Customized_Work_bench          = WA_SYT0060_TRC-CUSWB
        ) ).

PLease suggest the solution 

@Sandra_Rossi 

@SAPSupport 

@RaymondGiuseppi

entry.title: Distribute amount to payment date based on invoice date using SAC data action script
entry.content:
I have use case for manual forecasting from excel sheet to SAC where based on each invoice date needs to have 30 days in payment date having same invoice amount for each payment date. I tried using FOR EACH AND NEXT but it not as per expected format. Attached screenshot. Thank you

Note: We are using Invoice date and payment date in model. Happy to get ideas on how DSO can replace the manual way of forecasting.

entry.title: Issue with Importing Public Sector Management Planning Standard Content – No Demo Data
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I tried to import the Public Sector Management Planning standard content into SAP Analytics Cloud using the "Overwrite Objects and Data" option. However, the data is not appearing in the associated data model — it shows as empty.

Please see the attached screenshots for reference.

I was expecting to see the SAP demo data as shown in the SAP Help documentation (link attached). Could anyone  kindly assist in resolving this issue? I would like to view the demo data as intended.

Helpdoc: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/42093f14b43c485fbe3adbbe81eff6c8/7a23be9875ab475bb67855f5ed3c31a3.html?locale=en-US

Models:
Revenue Planning

Project planning:

Quick support Please ,Thank you!

Regards,
Sowmiya

entry.title: Re: Issue in UNIT_CONVERSION in CDS View Entity
entry.content:
Hello JR,

this happens because even when the UoM is defined, the system doesn't know how many pieces (ST) will be a carton (KAR) in general (I think this happens for the entries marked as "dimensionless", a conversion of a weight or a length for example should be successful here). Please check if you have entries in the MARM table for the materials you'd like to convert.

Check out this blog post about how to perform a unit conversion based on MARM, maybe it helps you for your case: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/material-quantity-unit-conversion-using-cds-views/ba-p/13365950

entry.title: Re: How to connect On-Premise system to SAP BTP
entry.content:
To connect your on-premise system to SAP BTP, you need to use the SAP Cloud Connector, which securely bridges your internal network (Intranet) with the SAP BTP (Internet). This allows BTP services to access selected on-premise resources without exposing the entire system. The Cloud Connector acts as a reverse proxy, ensuring controlled and secure access. Refer to this https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IgjEEKvjygU  for a step-by-step setup.

Thank you 
Danesh Naik

entry.title: Re: Best way to do Extensibility for Digital Signature in...
entry.content: Many tks Chuma. we will follow this path. Best regards CR

entry.title: Re: Best way to do Extensibility for Digital Signature in Public for Angola
entry.content: Dear Miguel. We have already contacted AGT to clarify the steps that must be taken for Public Cloud certification. This clarification is important because we do not exclude any option. Yes, we can use the logic in S/4 and adapt it via DRC in Public or consume another certification engine, using the DRC framework in the same way. I will update. Many tks. Best regards CR

entry.title: Unable to edit ARIBA PR after PO creation.
entry.content:
PR and PO is created in ARIBA and in SAP with help of BAPI.

When trying to change the PR in ARIBA, the SAP is throwing the error SE721.

entry.title: Re: Unable to edit ARIBA PR after PO creation.
entry.content: Change the error to warning

entry.title: Re: Blocked sales order not appearing in UKM_MY_DCDs & UK...
entry.content: I encountered the same issue in my S/4 system. In transaction codes VKM* and table UKM_DCD_ATTR, as well as the Fiori app “Manage Documented Credit Decisionscan,” all can display the blocked sales orders (SOs). However, in transaction code UKM_MY_DCD, there is no display. I would like UKM_MY_DCD to show the blocked SOs. How can I achieve this? Could someone please guide me? More details are provided in the attached image. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: When adding AP Invoice "Reconciliation difference must be zero before reconciling [Message 3821-8]"
entry.content:
Hi All,

My AP Invoice is in draft mode, but when going to add the document, system is showing this error "Reconciliation difference must be zero before reconciling [Message 3821-8]"


Please assists do add the document.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Shift defination to accommodate overtime pro...
entry.content:
Hi, you can define 24hrs shifts that span the entire day. For periods without production, you can log a scheduled downtime. This approach ensures that OEE KPIs accurately reflect production performance, including overtime, without skewing the metrics.

Flexible shift definitions can be accommodated through the configuration of multiple shifts that collectively cover the full day. You can also log scheduled downtime during buffer periods when overproduction is not occurring, ensuring KPI values remain accurate.

Alternatively, if defining full-day shifts is not feasible, you may configure shifts that include a buffer for potential overtime. During non-production periods, scheduled downtime can be logged to maintain data integrity and prevent distortion in KPI calculations.

Hope it helps,
Manoel
